select_when: Hidden user, stakeholder, or teammate constraints will determine whether a solution is trusted, adopted, or resisted.
Observation, interviews, or reflection are being used to challenge assumptions rather than merely decorate a decision that is already set.
A message, service, or intervention must fit another person's actual mental model, language, fear, and motivation.
The work would improve if perspective-taking surfaced tensions between what people say, do, fear, and hope.
avoid_when: Emotional identification is starting to compromise strategic clarity or crowd out the need to protect legitimate interests.
The model is being used to simulate care or intimacy without the observation, reflection, or validation work needed to make understanding real.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on perspective-taking, hidden constraints, and adoption-relevant understanding rather than widening it into generic kindness rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor human-centered-design sprawl beyond the raw source's stronger perspective-taking, empathy-map, and anti-manipulation surface.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch remains Wave 1 to Wave 3 only.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that spans design thinking, leadership, sales, and communication strategy.

select_when.note: The source is explicit that empathy is strongest when hidden user constraints and perspective fit determine whether a solution will matter or be adopted.

avoid_when.note: The limitation section directly warns about empathy compromising strategic clarity or becoming manipulative if it loses rigor.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on stakeholder-perspective fit problems, while the compact type label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source yields better reframing and trust-building through perspective fit, but the typed output label is a compact reviewed abstraction.

reasoning_types.note: The model is naturally diagnostic of human constraints and causal in how perspective fit changes adoption, while the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
